fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer audiologist license to minnesota, audiologist compact minnesota, minnesota audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathologist, Audiologist, and Hearing Instrument Dispenser Advisory Council
board.mailing_address:
Minnesota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
P.O. Box 64966
St. Paul, MN 55164-0966
board.physical_address: 85 E. 7th Place, Suite 220, St. Paul, MN 55164

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Minnesota.

steps.title: Create an Online Services Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Minnesota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology's Online Services portal and register as a 'New User' to create an account. This account will be used to submit and manage your application.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Application Form
steps.description: Log into your Online Services account, select 'Application for Audiologist Licensure,' and complete all required sections of the online application form. Ensure all personal, educational, and professional details are accurate.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents through the Online Services portal, which may include official transcripts, proof of supervised professional practice, verification of national certification (e.g., ASHA CCC-A), and any other supporting materials as required by the board.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee through the Online Services portal using a credit card. The current application fee for an Audiologist license is $150.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once the complete application and all supporting documents are received, the Minnesota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology will review your submission. The board may contact you for additional information if needed.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Minnesota
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Minnesota, applicants must submit their application through the Minnesota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Online Services portal.

official_links.portal_name: Minnesota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Online Services
official_links.registration_steps: To create an account and start an application, navigate to the 'Online Services' button on the applicants page or the board's homepage. Select 'New Users' and follow the prompts to register. Once registered, log in and select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Application for Audiologist Licensure').
